REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT AUDREY DER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. MOVING PARTY FAILED TO CARRY ITS BURDEN TO ESTABLISH ENTITLEMENT TO A JUDGMENT AS A MATTER OF LAW. THERE IS NO AUTHORITY AND NO EVIDENCE THAT (1) THE RENT BOARD WAS TASKED WITH A DETERMINATION OF WHETHER DEFENDANT'S REPRESENTATIONS REGARDING THE OCCUPANCY OF THE UNIT WAS INTENTIONALLY OR NEGLIGENTLY MISLEADING; OR (2) THAT THE RENT BOARD HAS JURISDICTION TO DETERMINE WHETHER SUCH REPRESENTATIONS WERE INTENTIONALLY OR NEGLIGENTLY MISLEADING. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: KENNETH B. HUNT; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Nov-09-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT AUDREY DER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is DENIED. Moving party failed to carry its burden to establish entitlement to a judgment as a matter of law. There is no authority and no evidence that (1) the Rent Board was tasked with a determination of whether Defendant's representations regarding the occupancy of the unit was intentionally or negligently misleading; or (2) that the Rent Board has jurisdiction to determine whether such representations were intentionally or negligently misleading.

Order signed. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

Defendant And Crosscomplainant Audrey Der???S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories And Requests For Production Of Documents; And For An Award Of Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-24-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Jessica Alexandra; Alexandra Law Firm; 415-350-9795; appearing on behalf of defendant and cross-complainant Audrey Der.
Stanley W. Riddell; 415-787-0576; appearing on behalf of plaintiff Keith Goodman.

Plaintiff contests the court's tentative ruling. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT AND CROSS-COMPLAINANT AUDREY DER'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES AND REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS; AND FOR AN AWARD OF SANCTIONS is GRANTED. Within 15 days of notice of entry of order Plaintiff to provide complete verified responses without objections to Special Interrogatories Nos. 9, 10, 21, and 35 and to Request for Production of Documents Nos. 8, 17, 24, and 26. Sanctions granted as requested to be paid within 30 days of notice of entry of order.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Sep-16-2022 Continued To Nov-29-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Arbitration
Off Calendar Sep-06-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: CROSS DEFENDANT ANDREW DER, JUNE KWAN, BEVERLY YOUNG, DAISY UNG Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Arbitration is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Request to compel arbitration is DENIED. Moving party failed to show that the cross-complaint for indemnity is "a dispute covered by the agreement." Furthermore, there is pending litigation with a third party with a potential for conflicting ruling on common issues of law and fact (habitability cross-complaint). CCP 1281.2(c). Moving party failed to cite any authority for the proposition that two parties may contract around this provision and failed to provide convincing argument that April 22, 2021 stipulation in fact contemplated third-party cross-complaint that has not been filed until September 15, 2021.
However, April 22, 2021 stipulation does appear to indicate the parties' intention not to proceed with "any" litigation in San Francisco Superior Court until pending arbitration is resolved. Therefore, Cross-Complaint for indemnity is stayed until the completion of the pending Arbitration proceedings (assuming they have not been completed as of the date of this order). Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (D501)
